prevalence.trend: stable; underdiagnosis and misclassification as TIA/stroke common

burden.dalyPerCapita: low at population level; high per-case disability during attacks

vulnerablePopulations: patients with recurrent aura and neurologic deficits
patients with limited access to neurology and imaging
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: ICHD-3: migraine with aura including motor weakness; differentiate from stroke/TIA; neuroimaging typically required at first presentation

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; depends on specialist diagnosis and preventive therapy access

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Often evaluated emergently as possible stroke; neurology follow-up needed for definitive classification
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Acute management individualized; avoid vasoconstrictors in some cases; preventive therapy (e.g., verapamil, acetazolamide) in specialist care
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Misdiagnosis leading to unnecessary stroke workups and limited specialist headache services outside large centers
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Access to emergency imaging in larger hospitals enables safe exclusion of stroke
